hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_title: Telling the Story of a Project -- Beginning, Development, and Current Status
lesson.menu_desc: Tell an international partner the story of a Sebrae project: how it started, how it grew over time, and where it is right now -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 18 -- Telling the Story of a Project
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 18 -- Telling the Story of a Project
lesson.grammar_point: sequencing a project narrative (started, over time, right now, our goal is)

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: the act of putting a plan into real action
the process of growing bigger or reaching more places
the basic structure of ideas that supports a plan
how big a project is and what it includes
the order of dates and steps in a project
the final result of a project or action
one part or stage of a longer project
the official start of a project or product
new information about how a project is going

reading.rtitle: Flavia Tells the Story of Her Project
paras: Flavia is at an international meeting. A partner asks about one of her projects, so she tells the story from the beginning. 'We started this project in 2020, with a small framework and a clear scope. At first, we worked in only three cities.' A good story has a clear beginning.

Then she describes the development. 'Over time, the project grew. We followed the timeline step by step, and the implementation of each phase brought good progress. The first outcome was strong: more than two hundred small businesses joined us.' She shows how the project moved from one phase to the next.

Right now, the project is in a new phase. 'We are in the expansion phase, and we share a monthly update with our partners,' she says. 'Our goal is a national launch, to reach every state by 2027.' The partner is impressed by the clear story, from the beginning to the current status.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about telling the story of a project: beginning, development, and current status

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Flavia cannot remember when her project started, so she says nothing.
Flavia tells the story of her project in a clear order: the beginning, the development, and the current status.
The text explains how to buy a plane ticket to an international meeting.

items: The text says: 'We started this project in 2020, with a small framework and a clear scope.'
The project stayed in only three cities and never grew.
The text says: 'Over time, the project grew. We followed the timeline step by step.'
Right now, the project is in the expansion phase.
The text says: 'We are in the expansion phase, and we share a monthly update with our partners.'
Flavia's goal is to close the project in 2027.
The text says: 'Our goal is a national launch, to reach every state by 2027.'

parts: . Right now, the project is in the

gapfill.text: Tell a project story in order, with time markers. Use started or at first for the beginning, over time or then for the development, right now or currently for the present, and our goal is for the future. These markers make your story easy to follow.

bank.label: Useful sentences to tell the story of a project
items: We started this project in 2020, with a small framework.
At first, the scope was small: only three cities.
Over time, the project grew, phase by phase.
We followed the timeline, and the implementation brought good progress.
Right now, we are in the expansion phase.
We share a monthly update with our partners.
Our goal is a national launch, to reach every state by 2027.

lf.title: Complete the project story
items: , the project grew, phase by phase.
, we are in the expansion phase.

lf.text: Use these markers to order your story in time: started or at first for the beginning, over time or then for the development, right now or currently for the present, and our goal is for the future.

items.situation: A partner asks how your project began. Tell the beginning.
tips: We started this project in 2020, with a small framework.
At first, the scope was small -- only three cities.

items.situation: The partner asks how the project developed. Describe the middle.
tips: Over time, the project grew, phase by phase.
We followed the timeline, and each phase brought good progress.

items.situation: The partner asks about the current status. Say where you are now.
tips: Right now, we are in the expansion phase.
Currently, we share a monthly update with our partners.

items.situation: The partner asks about the future. Share your goal.
tips: Our goal is a national launch by 2027.
Our goal is to reach every state in the country.

items: Tell the story of a Sebrae project from the start: when it started, its scope, and its first framework. Use 'We started...' and 'At first...'.
Describe how the project grew over time: the timeline, the phases, and the progress. Use 'Over time...' and 'phase by phase'.
Say where the project is right now: the current phase, and the monthly update. Use 'Right now...' or 'Currently...'.
Tell the full story in four steps -- beginning, development, current status, and goal -- with no notes.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Beginning: 'We started this project in 2020, with a small framework and a clear scope.'
Development: 'Over time, the project grew, and the implementation of each phase brought good progress.'
Current status: 'Right now, we are in the expansion phase, and we share a monthly update.'
Goal: 'Our goal is a national launch, to reach every state by 2027.'
answerkey.note: A clear project story moves in time: beginning, development, current status, and goal.

listenings.text: Let me tell you the story of our project. We started it in 2020, with a small framework and a clear scope. At first, we worked in only three cities. Over time, the project grew. We followed the timeline, phase by phase, and the implementation of each phase brought good progress. The first outcome was strong: more than two hundred small businesses joined us. Right now, we are in the expansion phase, and we share a monthly update with our partners. Our goal is a national launch, to reach every state by 2027.

Here is one simple tip to tell the story of a project in English. Use time markers to order your story. For the beginning, say: we started the project in 2020. For the development, say: over time, the project grew. For the present, say: right now, we are in the expansion phase. And for the future, say: our goal is a national launch. Beginning, development, now, and goal. Four clear steps, and your story is easy to follow. Good luck!

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia tells the beginning and says, we started the project in 2020, with a clear scope. Then, she describes the development and says, over time, the project grew, phase by phase. Next, she talks about the first outcome and the progress the project made. After that, she describes the current status and says, right now, we are in the expansion phase. Finally, she shares her goal, a national launch to reach every state by 2027.
